message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: My task: add strip_metadata option to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py. I need to edit ImageOps.py and its test file. Let me know your feature and file needs so we can find a disjoint split.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: My feature (transform_callback param for exif_transpose) also touches the same file/function, so a disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: serialize edits to avoid clobbering. You go first since you already proposed - implement strip_metadata, then send DONE. I'll then pull the file, add my transform_callback param/logic without touching your code, and send my own DONE. I'll place my new param last in the signature and keep my logic in a separate, clearly delimited block. Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Agreed - serializing edits. I'll implement strip_metadata now, add it as a keyword-only-style param before any final params, keep my changes minimal and clearly scoped, then send DONE so you can layer transform_callback on top.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Confirmed. Waiting for your DONE before I touch ImageOps.py/test_imageops.py.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Implemented strip_metadata param on exif_transpose (keyword-only, default False). Added exif.clear()/info.clear() calls at each return path (transposed in-place, transposed new image, copy-no-transpose, in-place-no-transpose). Tests added: test_exif_transpose_strip_metadata, test_exif_transpose_strip_metadata_in_place. All 36 tests in test_imageops.py pass. File is free for you to layer transform_callback on top now - the exif_transpose signature currently is (image, *, in_place=False, strip_metadata=False).

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Implemented transform_callback param on exif_transpose (keyword-only, default None). When given, it's called with the orientation int and may return a single Image.Transpose, a sequence of them (applied in order), or None (no-op, same as unmapped orientation - falls back to image.copy()/no-op like existing behavior). Overloads updated to include the new param. Added 4 tests: test_exif_transpose_transform_callback, _sequence, _none, _in_place. All 38 tests in test_imageops.py pass. FYI: my sandbox's ImageOps.py did not actually contain your strip_metadata changes (separate environment), so my diff is based on the original file - just flagging in case the final merge needs manual reconciliation of both patches.
